session.user: what models are used for remote dispatch right now

tool_use.name: Agent
tool_use.param:
FIRST check docs/learned/index.md for existing documentation on this topic. Read relevant ...[truncated 426 chars]...ection for dispatch
Be thorough - check workflows, CLI code, and any configuration files.

session.tool_result:
Excellent! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me compile the final report:
## Summary
I've completed a thorough search of the erk codebase for AI model configurations used in remote dispatch. Here are my findings:
### Model Configuration Locations
#### 1. **GitHub Actions Workflows** (Primary Configuration Points)
All dispatch-related workflows define `model_name` as a workflow input with a standardized default:
| Workflow | Location | Default Model | Input Parameter | |----------|----------|---|---| | **one-shot.yml** | `/Users/schrockn/code/erk/.github/workflows/one-shot.yml` | `claude-opus-4-6` | `model_name` (line 27-31) | | **plan-implement.yml** | `/Users/schrockn/code/erk/.github/workflows/plan-implement.yml` | `claude-opus-4-6` | `model_name` (line 31-35 & 77-81) | | **pr-address.yml** | `/Users/schrockn/code/erk/.github/workflows/pr-address.yml` | `claude-opus-4-6` | `model_name` (line 15-19) | | **pr-rebase.yml** | `/Users/schrockn/code/erk/.github/workflows/pr-rebase.yml` | `claude-opus-4-6` | `model_name` (default specified) | | **pr-rewrite.yml** | `/Users/schrockn/code/erk/.github/workflows/pr-rewrite.yml` | `claude-opus-4-6` | `model_name` (line 23-27) | | **consolidate-learn-plans.yml** | `/Users/schrockn/code/erk/.github/workflows/consolidate-learn-plans.yml` | `claude-opus-4-6` | `model_name` (line 23-27) | | **learn.yml** | `/Users/schrockn/code/erk/.github/workflows/learn.yml` | `claude-opus-4-6` | Hard-coded (line 71) |
#### 2. **Repository Configuration** (.erk/config.toml)
Location: `/Users/schrockn/code/erk/.erk/config.toml`
Contains workflow-specific model overrides: ```toml [workflows.erk-impl] model_name = "claude-opus-4-5"
... [92 lines omitted] ...
session.assistant:
All remote dispatch workflows default to **`claude-opus-4-6`**. Here's the breakdown:
**Workflow defaults** (all 7 Claude-invoking workflows): | Workflow | Default Model | |---|---| | one-shot.yml | `claude-opus-4-6` | | plan-implement.yml | `claude-opus-4-6` | | pr-address.yml | `claude-opus-4-6` | | pr-rebase.yml | `claude-opus-4-6` | | pr-rewrite.yml | `claude-opus-4-6` | | consolidate-learn-plans.yml | `claude-opus-4-6` | | learn.yml | `claude-opus-4-6` (hard-coded) |
**Override chain** (highest to lowest priority): 1. CLI `--model` flag (e.g., `erk one-shot --model sonnet`) 2. `.erk/config.toml` per-workflow config — currently has `model_name = "claude-opus-4-5"` for `erk-impl` 3. Workflow YAML default (`claude-opus-4-6`)
**Available model aliases** (CLI shorthand): - `o` / `opus` → `claude-opus-4-6` - `s` / `sonnet` → `claude-sonnet-4-6` - `h` / `haiku` → `claude-haiku-4-5-20251001`
So in practice: `plan-implement` dispatches use **opus 4.5** (via config.toml override), while everything else uses **opus 4.6**.
Let me read the workflow files to understand the exact changes needed.
session.tool_result:
1→name: one-shot 2→run-name: "one-shot${{ inputs.plan_only && '(plan)' || '' }}:#${{ inputs.pr_number }}:${{ inputs.distinct_id }}" 3→ 4→on: 5→ workflow_dispatch: 6→ inputs: 7→ prompt: 8→ description: "Task description for autonomous execution" 9→ required: true 10→ type: string 11→ branch_name: 12→ description: "Branch to work on (created by CLI command)" 13→ required: true 14→ type: string 15→ pr_number: 16→ description: "Draft PR number (created by CLI command)" 17→ required: true 18→ type: string 19→ submitted_by: 20→ description: "GitHub username of the person who submitted" 21→ required: true 22→ type: string 23→ distinct_id: 24→ description: "Unique identifier for run discovery (base36)" 25→ required: true 26→ type: string 27→ model_name: 28→ description: "Claude model to use for execution" 29→ required: false 30→ type: string
... [10 lines omitted] ...

1→name: plan-implement 2→run-name: "${{ inputs.dispatch_type == 'incremental' && '[incremental] ' || '' }}${{ inputs.branch_name }} (#${{ inputs.pr_number }}):${{ inputs.distinct_id }}" 3→ 4→on: 5→ workflow_dispatch: 6→ inputs: 7→ plan_id: 8→ description: "Plan identifier to implement" 9→ required: true 10→ type: string 11→ submitted_by: 12→ description: "GitHub username of the person who submitted the issue" 13→ required: true 14→ type: string 15→ distinct_id: 16→ description: "Unique identifier for run discovery (base36)" 17→ required: true 18→ type: string 19→ plan_title: 20→ description: "Plan title for workflow run display" 21→ required: true 22→ type: string 23→ branch_name: 24→ description: "Branch name for implementation (created by submit command)" 25→ required: true 26→ type: string 27→ pr_number: 28→ description: "PR number for implementation (created by submit command)" 29→ required: true 30→ type: string
... [60 lines omitted] ...

1→name: pr-address 2→run-name: "pr-address:#${{ inputs.pr_number }}:${{ inputs.distinct_id }}" 3→ 4→on: 5→ workflow_dispatch: 6→ inputs: 7→ pr_number: 8→ description: "PR number to address review comments" 9→ required: true 10→ type: string 11→ distinct_id: 12→ description: "Unique identifier for run discovery (base36)" 13→ required: true 14→ type: string 15→ model_name: 16→ description: "Claude model to use" 17→ required: false 18→ type: string 19→ default: "claude-opus-4-6" 20→concurrency: 21→ group: pr-address-${{ github.event.inputs.pr_number }} 22→ cancel-in-progress: true 23→ 24→jobs: 25→ address: 26→ if: vars.CLAUDE_ENABLED != 'false' 27→ runs-on: ubuntu-latest 28→ timeout-minutes: 30 29→ permissions: 30→ contents: write

1→name: pr-rebase 2→run-name: "rebase:#${{ inputs.pr_number }}:${{ inputs.distinct_id }}" 3→ 4→on: 5→ workflow_dispatch: 6→ inputs: 7→ branch_name: 8→ description: "Branch to rebase" 9→ required: true 10→ type: string 11→ base_branch: 12→ description: "Base branch to rebase onto" 13→ required: true 14→ type: string 15→ pr_number: 16→ description: "PR number for status updates" 17→ required: true 18→ type: string 19→ distinct_id: 20→ description: "Unique identifier for run discovery (base36)" 21→ required: true 22→ type: string 23→ squash: 24→ description: "Whether to squash commits before rebase" 25→ required: false 26→ type: boolean 27→ default: true 28→ model_name: 29→ description: "Claude model to use for conflict resolution" 30→ required: false

1→name: pr-rewrite 2→run-name: "rewrite:#${{ inputs.pr_number }}:${{ inputs.distinct_id }}" 3→ 4→on: 5→ workflow_dispatch: 6→ inputs: 7→ branch_name: 8→ description: "Branch to rewrite" 9→ required: true 10→ type: string 11→ base_branch: 12→ description: "Base branch to rebase onto" 13→ required: true 14→ type: string 15→ pr_number: 16→ description: "PR number for status updates" 17→ required: true 18→ type: string 19→ distinct_id: 20→ description: "Unique identifier for run discovery (base36)" 21→ required: true 22→ type: string 23→ model_name: 24→ description: "Claude model to use for conflict resolution" 25→ required: false 26→ type: string 27→ default: "claude-opus-4-6" 28→concurrency: 29→ group: rewrite-${{ github.event.inputs.branch_name }} 30→ cancel-in-progress: true
... [5 lines omitted] ...

1→name: consolidate-learn-plans 2→run-name: "consolidate-learn-plans:#${{ inputs.pr_number }}:${{ inputs.distinct_id }}" 3→ 4→on: 5→ workflow_dispatch: 6→ inputs: 7→ branch_name: 8→ description: "Branch to work on (created by CLI command)" 9→ required: true 10→ type: string 11→ pr_number: 12→ description: "Draft PR number (created by CLI command)" 13→ required: true 14→ type: string 15→ submitted_by: 16→ description: "GitHub username of the person who submitted" 17→ required: true 18→ type: string 19→ distinct_id: 20→ description: "Unique identifier for run discovery (base36)" 21→ required: true 22→ type: string 23→ model_name: 24→ description: "Claude model to use for execution" 25→ required: false 26→ type: string 27→ default: "claude-opus-4-6" 28→ 29→concurrency: 30→ group: consolidate-learn-plans-${{ inputs.branch_name }}
... [5 lines omitted] ...

1→# Learn Dispatch Workflow 2→# 3→# Runs /erk:learn on a completed plan to extract insights and patterns 4→# from the implementation session. This workflow is triggered asynchronously 5→# after a PR lands via the upload-impl-session pipeline. 6→# 7→# Output: Creates a new learn plan issue containing extracted lessons learned, 8→# architectural decisions, and reusable patterns from the implementation. 9→ 10→name: learn 11→run-name: "${{ inputs.pr_number }}:${{ inputs.distinct_id }}" 12→ 13→on: 14→ workflow_dispatch: 15→ inputs: 16→ pr_number: 17→ description: "PR number to learn from" 18→ required: true 19→ type: string 20→ distinct_id: 21→ description: "Unique identifier for run discovery (base36)" 22→ required: true 23→ type: string 24→ learn_branch: 25→ description: "Branch containing learn materials in .erk/impl-context/" 26→ required: true 27→ type: string 28→ plan_backend: 29→ description: "Plan backend type (github or planned_pr)" 30→ required: false
... [49 lines omitted] ...

1→# work config for this repository 2→# Available template variables: {worktree_path}, {repo_root}, {name} 3→ 4→[env] 5→# EXAMPLE_KEY = "{worktree_path}" 6→ 7→[post_create] 8→# shell = "bash" 9→# commands = [ 10→# "uv venv", 11→# "uv run make dev_install", 12→# ] 13→ 14→[workflows.erk-impl] 15→model_name = "claude-opus-4-5" 16→ 17→[pool] 18→max_slots = 64 19→

1→--- 2→title: Workflow Model Policy 3→last_audited: "2026-02-17 00:00 PT" 4→audit_result: clean 5→read_when: 6→ - "creating or modifying GitHub Actions workflows that invoke Claude" 7→ - "choosing which Claude model to use in a workflow" 8→ - "understanding why all workflows default to Opus" 9→tripwires: 10→ - action: "creating workflows that invoke Claude without specifying model" 11→ warning: "All workflows MUST default to claude-opus-4-6. See workflow-model-policy.md for the standardization rationale." 12→--- 13→ 14→# Workflow Model Policy 15→ 16→All erk GitHub Actions workflows that invoke Claude MUST default to `claude-opus-4-6`. This standardization was completed across all 6 Claude-invoking workflows to ensure consistent behavior and eliminate per-workflow model selection confusion. 17→ 18→## Affected Workflows 19→ 20→| Workflow | Model Parameter | Default | 21→| -------------------- | ------------------------- | ----------------- | 22→| `ci.yml` | Direct reference | `claude-opus-4-6` | 23→| `learn.yml` | Direct reference | `claude-opus-4-6` | 24→| `one-shot.yml` | `model_name` input | `claude-opus-4-6` | 25→| `plan-implement.yml` | `model_name` input (dual) | `claude-opus-4-6` | 26→| `pr-address.yml` | `model_name` input | `claude-opus-4-6` | 27→| `pr-rebase.yml` | `model_name` input | `claude-opus-4-6` | 28→ 29→Workflows without Claude invocation (`docs.yml`, `code-reviews.yml`) are not affected. 30→
... [25 lines omitted] ...

session.tool_result:
PreToolUse:ExitPlanMode hook error: [command -v erk >/dev/null 2>&1 || exit 0; ERK_HOOK_ID=exit-plan-mode-hook erk exec exit-plan-mode-hook]: DISPLAY PLAN: Before asking the question below, read the plan file and display its contents to the user with proper markdown formatting: /Users/schrockn/.claude/plans/pure-tinkering-finch.md
PLAN SAVE PROMPT
A plan exists for this session but has not been saved.
Use AskUserQuestion to ask the user: question: "📋 Switch all remote dispatch workflows to Sonnet 4.6 Current context: (wt:erk) (br:master)\n\nWhat would you like to do with this plan?" header: "br:master"
IMPORTANT: Present options in this exact order: 1. "Create new branch and planned PR. Stays on current branch." - Create a new branch, save plan as draft PR. 2. "Implement without saving" - Implement directly on the current branch without saving a plan PR. 3. "View/Edit the Plan" - Open plan in editor to review or modify before deciding.
⚠️ WARNING: Currently on 'master'. We strongly discourage implementing directly on the trunk branch. Consider saving the plan and implementing in a dedicated worktree instead.
If user chooses 'Create new branch and planned PR': 1. Run /erk:plan-save 2. Call ExitPlanMode to end the planning session.
If user chooses 'Implement without saving': 1. Create implement-now marker: erk exec marker create --session-id 9095c37e-8641-4780-b78e-7424932bbe2f \ exit-plan-mode-hook.implement-now 2. Call ExitPlanMode 3. After exiting plan mode, implement the changes directly on the current branch. Do NOT run 'erk exec setup-impl' or create a new branch.
... [8 lines omitted] ...

1→# Switch all remote dispatch workflows to Sonnet 4.6 2→ 3→## Context 4→ 5→All 7 Claude-invoking workflows currently default to `claude-opus-4-6`. Additionally, `.erk/config.toml` overrides plan-implement to `claude-opus-4-5`. Switching everything to `claude-sonnet-4-6` for cost/speed optimization. 6→ 7→## Changes 8→ 9→### 1. Workflow YAML defaults (`claude-opus-4-6` → `claude-sonnet-4-6`) 10→ 11→Each file has a `default: "claude-opus-4-6"` line to change: 12→ 13→- `.github/workflows/one-shot.yml` (line 31) 14→- `.github/workflows/plan-implement.yml` (line 35 + line 81 — dual default for workflow_dispatch and workflow_call) 15→- `.github/workflows/pr-address.yml` (line 19) 16→- `.github/workflows/pr-rebase.yml` (line 32) 17→- `.github/workflows/pr-rewrite.yml` (line 27) 18→- `.github/workflows/consolidate-learn-plans.yml` (line 27) 19→- `.github/workflows/learn.yml` (line 71 — hard-coded `--model claude-opus-4-6` in bash) 20→ 21→### 2. Config override (`.erk/config.toml`) 22→ 23→Line 15: `model_name = "claude-opus-4-5"` → `model_name = "claude-sonnet-4-6"` 24→ 25→### 3. Policy doc update (`docs/learned/ci/workflow-model-policy.md`) 26→ 27→Update the policy to reflect `claude-sonnet-4-6` as the new standard default across all workflows and tripwire text. 28→ 29→## Verification 30→
... [3 lines omitted] ...
session.assistant:
## Plan: Switch all remote dispatch workflows to Sonnet 4.6
### Context
All 7 Claude-invoking workflows currently default to `claude-opus-4-6`. Additionally, `.erk/config.toml` overrides plan-implement to `claude-opus-4-5`. Switching everything to `claude-sonnet-4-6`.
### Changes
**1. Workflow YAML defaults** (`claude-opus-4-6` → `claude-sonnet-4-6`)
- `.github/workflows/one-shot.yml` (line 31) - `.github/workflows/plan-implement.yml` (line 35 + line 81 — dual default) - `.github/workflows/pr-address.yml` (line 19) - `.github/workflows/pr-rebase.yml` (line 32) - `.github/workflows/pr-rewrite.yml` (line 27) - `.github/workflows/consolidate-learn-plans.yml` (line 27) - `.github/workflows/learn.yml` (line 71 — hard-coded `--model` in bash)
**2. Config override** (`.erk/config.toml`): `claude-opus-4-5` → `claude-sonnet-4-6`
**3. Policy doc update** (`docs/learned/ci/workflow-model-policy.md`)
### Verification
- `grep -r "claude-opus" .github/workflows/` should return no results - `grep "model_name" .erk/config.toml` should show `claude-sonnet-4-6`

tool_use.name: AskUserQuestion
tool_use.param: [{'question': '📋 Switch all remote dispatch workflows to Sonnet 4.6\nCurrent context: (wt:...[truncated 403 chars]...ion': 'Open plan in editor to review or modify before deciding.'}], 'multiSelect': False}]

session.tool_result:
User has answered your questions: "📋 Switch all remote dispatch workflows to Sonnet 4.6 Current context: (wt:erk) (br:master)
What would you like to do with this plan?"="Create new branch and planned PR". You can now continue with the user's answers in mind.
session.user:
# /erk:plan-save
Save the current session's plan to GitHub with session context.
## Usage
```bash /erk:plan-save # Standalone plan /erk:plan-save --plan-type=learn # Learn plan (erk-learn label) /erk:plan-save --objective=123 # Explicit objective link (overrides marker) /erk:plan-save --current-branch # Use current branch instead of creating new plnd/ branch ```
Objective linking: if a plan was created via `/erk:objective-plan`, the session's objective-context marker is read automatically by the save command. Use `--objective=<number>` to override the marker or provide the link when no marker exists (e.g., during replan).
## Plan Storage
The plan is saved using the configured backend:
- **Draft PR backend** (`PLAN_BACKEND = "draft_pr"`): Creates a branch, pushes a plan commit, and opens a draft PR. Plan content is in the PR body after the metadata separator. The JSON output contract includes `pr_number`, `pr_url`, `title`, `branch_name`, `plan_backend`.
## Agent Instructions
### Step 1: Parse Arguments
Check `` for the `--plan-type`, `--objective`, and `--current-branch` flags:
``` If contains "--plan-type=<type>": - Extract the type (standard or learn) - Store as PLAN_TYPE variable - Set PLAN_TYPE_FLAG to "--plan-type=<type>" Else: - Set PLAN_TYPE_FLAG to empty string
If contains "--objective=<number>": - Extract the number - Set OBJECTIVE_FLAG to "--objective=<number>" Else: - Set OBJECTIVE_FLAG to empty string
If contains "--current-branch": - Set CURRENT_BRANCH_FLAG to "--current-branch" Else: - Set CURRENT_BRANCH_FLAG to empty string ```
### Step 2: Generate Branch Slug
**Skip this step if `--current-branch` was passed.** The current branch is used directly.
Before saving, generate a branch slug from the plan title. Read the plan title from the plan file you wrote in plan mode (the first `# ` heading).
Generate a branch slug from the title:
- 2-4 hyphenated lowercase words, max 30 characters - Capture distinctive essence, drop filler words (the, a, for, implementation, plan) - Prefer action verbs: add, fix, refactor, update, consolidate, extract, migrate - Examples: "fix-auth-session", "add-plan-validation", "refactor-gateway-abc"
Store the result as `BRANCH_SLUG`.
### Step 3: Generate Plan Summary
Write a concise 2-3 sentence summary of the plan. This summary will be visible at the top of the PR description (above the collapsed full plan).
Guidelines:
- Focus on WHAT the plan does and WHY - Do not repeat the title - Plain text, no markdown headers or formatting - Avoid special shell characters (backticks, dollar signs) - Store as PLAN_SUMMARY
### Step 4: Run Save Command
Run this command with the session ID, summary, and optional flags.
If CURRENT_BRANCH_FLAG is set, pass `--current-branch` (no branch slug needed):
```bash erk exec plan-save --format json --session-id="9095c37e-8641-4780-b78e-7424932bbe2f" --current-branch --summary="${PLAN_SUMMARY}" ${PLAN_TYPE_FLAG} ${OBJECTIVE_FLAG} ```
Otherwise, pass the branch slug:
```bash erk exec plan-save --format json --session-id="9095c37e-8641-4780-b78e-7424932bbe2f" --branch-slug="${BRANCH_SLUG}" --summary="${PLAN_SUMMARY}" ${PLAN_TYPE_FLAG} ${OBJECTIVE_FLAG} ```
Parse the JSON output to extract `pr_number` for verification in Step 5.
If the command fails, display the error and stop.
### Step 5: Verify Objective Link (if applicable)
**Only run this step if `objective_issue` is non-null in the JSON output from Step 4.**
Verify the objective link was saved correctly:
```bash erk exec get-plan-metadata <pr_number> objective_issue ```
Parse the JSON response:
- If `success: true` and `value` matches the expected objective number: verification passed - If `success: false` or value doesn't match: verification failed
**On verification success:**
Display: `Verified objective link: #<objective-number>`
**On verification failure:**
Display error and remediation steps:
``` ERROR: Objective link verification failed Expected objective: #<expected> Actual: <actual-or-null>
The plan was saved but without the correct objective link. Fix: Close draft PR #<pr_number>, ensure the objective-context marker exists, and re-run /erk:plan-save. ```
Exit without creating the plan-saved marker. The session continues so the user can retry.
### Step 6: Update Objective Roadmap (if objective linked)
**Only run this step if `objective_issue` was non-null in JSON output and verification passed.**
Update the objective's roadmap table to show that a plan has been created for this node:
1. **Read the roadmap node marker** to get the node ID:
```bash step_id=$(erk exec marker read --session-id "9095c37e-8641-4780-b78e-7424932bbe2f" roadmap-step) ```
If the marker doesn't exist (command fails), skip this step - the plan wasn't created via `objective-plan`.
2. **Update the roadmap table** using the dedicated command:
```bash erk exec update-objective-node <objective-issue> --node "$step_id" --pr "#<pr_number>" --status in_progress ```
This atomically fetches the objective body, finds the matching node row, sets the Status cell to `in-progress`, and writes the updated body back.
3. **Report the update:**
Display: `Updated objective #<objective-issue> roadmap: node <step_id> → PR #<pr_number>`
**Error handling:** If the roadmap update fails, warn but continue - the plan was saved successfully, just the roadmap tracking didn't update. The user can manually update the objective.
### Step 7: Display Results
**If JSON contains `skipped_duplicate: true`:**
Display: `Plan already saved as PR #<pr_number> (duplicate skipped)`
Then call ExitPlanMode. The exit-plan-mode hook will present "what next?" options.
**Otherwise, on success**, display:
``` Plan "<title>" saved as draft PR #<pr_number> URL: <issue_url> ```
If objective was verified, also display: `Verified objective link: #<objective-number>`
Then call ExitPlanMode. The exit-plan-mode hook will present "what next?" options (implement in current worktree, implement in new worktree, or done).
On failure, display the error message and suggest:
- Checking that a plan exists (enter Plan mode and exit it first) - Verifying GitHub CLI authentication (`gh auth status`) - Checking network connectivity
### Step 5: Push Planning Session
After saving successfully, push the planning session for cross-machine learning. This captures the in-progress session state so it's available even if implementation happens on a different machine.
Run `erk exec capture-session-info` to get the session file path, then push it:
```bash erk exec upload-impl-session --session-id "9095c37e-8641-4780-b78e-7424932bbe2f" 2>/dev/null || true ```
If the plan was saved successfully and `pr_number` is known, use push-session directly for better stage tracking:
```bash erk exec push-session \ --session-file "<session_file_path>" \ --session-id "9095c37e-8641-4780-b78e-7424932bbe2f" \ --stage planning \ --source local \ --pr-number <pr_number> \ 2>/dev/null || true ```
Where `<session_file_path>` is obtained from `erk exec capture-session-info` output and `<pr_number>` is from Step 2's JSON output.
**Note:** This is non-critical. If it fails, the plan was still saved successfully. The `2>/dev/null || true` ensures graceful degradation.
## Session Tracking
After successfully saving a plan, the plan number is stored in a marker file that enables automatic plan updates in the same session.
**To read the saved plan number:**
```bash erk exec marker read --session-id "9095c37e-8641-4780-b78e-7424932bbe2f" plan-saved-issue ```
This returns the plan number (exit code 0) or exits with code 1 if no plan was saved in this session.
session.assistant:
**Branch slug:** `switch-dispatch-to-sonnet`
**Plan summary:** Switches all 7 Claude-invoking GitHub Actions workflows and the config.toml override from Opus to claude-sonnet-4-6. Also updates the workflow model policy documentation to reflect the new default.

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: erk exec plan-save --format json --session-id="9095c37e-8641-4780-b78e-7424932bbe2f" --bra...[truncated 136 chars]...net-4-6. Also updates the workflow model policy documentation to reflect the new default."

session.tool_result: {"success": true, "pr_number": 9208, "pr_url": "https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/9208", "title": "[erk-pr] Switch all remote dispatch workflows to Sonnet 4.6", "branch_name": "plnd/switch-dispatch-to-sonnet-03-11-1151", "plan_backend": "planned_pr", "objective_issue": null, "archived_to": "/Users/schrockn/code/erk/.erk/scratch/sessions/9095c37e-8641-4780-b78e-7424932bbe2f/plan_snapshots/000001-85884c47"}

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: erk exec push-session \ --session-file "/Users/schrockn/.claude/projects/-Users-schroc...[truncated 96 chars]... --stage planning \ --source local \ --pr-number 9208 \ 2>/dev/null || true
